intake.risk_posture: medium; preserve behavior and route semantics, allow limited visual drift toward Timeline/D-zone/Database canon
intake.harness_policy: do not edit AGENTS.md or plugin skills in this tranche; queue learning/lint-law follow-ups if evidence appears
allowed_areas: focused tests/docs for the approved slice
protected_areas: database detail modal internals unless touched by a selected shared primitive
validation_commands: focused npx vitest run commands for touched areas
intake.stop_condition: stop when tranche is validated, same-concept scope expansion appears, validation fails twice, or product/design decision is needed

focus_areas.name: Impeccable extract/design-system normalization
focus_areas.evidence: User requested @impeccable extract; PRODUCT/DESIGN define Timeline and D-zone as canon and Database browse conventions as settled.
focus_areas.next: Scout/Judge first shared token or primitive slice.

focus_areas.name: Tailwind/CSS normalization
focus_areas.evidence: Target surfaces use overlapping slate/amber/radius/motion values through CSS vars, Tailwind arbitrary values, RGB, and OKLCH.

focus_areas.name: React/component composition
focus_areas.evidence: Timeline controls, D-zone alert switcher, and database controls repeat related product-control concepts with page-local markup.

focus_areas.name: UI/a11y
focus_areas.evidence: Focus, reduced motion, custom controls, card overlays, and responsive overflow must be preserved during migration.
focus_areas.next: Include in Judge invariants and browser checks.

focus_areas.name: Architecture/module boundaries
focus_areas.evidence: Shared UI vocabulary is desirable, but database card layout and Timeline/D-zone semantics should stay local.

focus_areas.name: Complexity hotspots
focus_areas.evidence: Complexity scan flags D-zone history, Timeline banner artwork, and database browse render-derived work inside or adjacent to scope.

candidates.title: Shared product-surface CSS tokens
candidates.concept_blast_radius: src/index.css or shared style entry; src/features/database/database.css; src/pages/timeline/timeline.css; src/pages/d-zone/d-zone.css
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W1. Added `--ui-*` product-surface tokens in src/index.css; mapped D-zone local vars to them; migrated Timeline and Database motion/focus/control styling to token references.
candidates.next: Implemented for the first tranche; future slices can migrate more component-level uses.

candidates.title: Shared compact chip/segmented control styling
candidates.concept_blast_radius: src/ui/filters/FilterChipButton.tsx; possible new shared primitive; Timeline price filter; D-zone alert switcher; database controls
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W2 for compact pressed/field control vocabulary. Added `.ui-compact-control` utilities in `src/index.css`; migrated FilterChipButton and database sort controls to shared classes; D-zone alert buttons now use shared pressed-control styling while keeping local alert behavior.
candidates.next: Timeline price toggle still has page-specific markup and can migrate to the same utility in a later Timeline slice if it remains behavior-compatible.

candidates.title: Shared section divider/header
candidates.concept_blast_radius: Timeline section headers; database browse row/header areas; possible src/ui primitive
candidates.evidence: Timeline has duplicate section heading shapes.
candidates.next: Queue unless first slice is smaller and safer.

candidates.title: Shared metadata token/line
candidates.concept_blast_radius: Timeline event/banner metadata; database card meta/stat areas; possible src/ui primitive
candidates.evidence: Repeated uppercase micro-label and compact metadata rows.
candidates.next: Needs Scout on intent differences before extraction.

candidates.title: Compact media action tile
candidates.concept_blast_radius: D-zone relic/monster buttons; database square-art/icon cards; src/ui/cards or feature adapters
candidates.evidence: Similar image well plus action target, but database hybrid cards are feature-owned.
candidates.next: Only implement if it does not globalize database card behavior.

candidates.title: D-zone history/browser complexity cleanup
candidates.evidence: Complexity scan flags long function, deep nesting, and repeated search in loop.
candidates.next: Later characterization unless Scout shows easy root fix.

candidates.title: Timeline banner artwork complexity cleanup
candidates.evidence: Complexity scan flags large file, branch-heavy, effects, inline style, arbitrary classes, outline-none.
candidates.next: Later characterization.

candidates.title: Database browse render-derived work
candidates.evidence: Performance scan flags render-derived collection work.
candidates.next: Queue unless directly relevant to selected UI primitive.

candidates.title: Neutral shared scrollbar utility
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W1. `.database-scrollbar` was a global utility with a database-specific name and was used by Timeline and D-zone; W1 added `.ui-scrollbar`, preserved the legacy alias, and migrated non-database/shared consumers.
candidates.next: Database detail internals keep the compatibility alias because they are protected in this goal.

candidates.title: Timeline active/upcoming/archive section renderer
candidates.evidence: Event and banner sections both partition active/upcoming/ended and render archive sections.
candidates.next: Queue for a later Timeline-local simplification slice.

candidates.title: Timeline banner tag/pricing composition helper
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W3. Added Timeline-local `BannerMetadataList` in `src/pages/timeline/bannerMetadata.tsx`; `BannerCardHero` uses it with `limit={4}` and `BannerInfoDrawer` uses it without a limit while preserving drawer empty-row spacing.
candidates.next: Implemented as Timeline-local root fix; no global metadata primitive extracted.

candidates.title: Shared compact disclosure row
candidates.concept_blast_radius: Timeline archive section; D-zone archive note/year toggles; possible src/ui primitive
candidates.evidence: Timeline and D-zone both use chevron disclosure rows with `aria-expanded`.
candidates.next: Needs Judge to confirm same interaction intent before extraction.

candidates.title: D-zone countdown helper
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W7. Added `getDZoneCountdownDisplay` in `d-zone-countdown.ts`, migrated current/history pages to it, and moved countdown tests to the helper boundary.
candidates.next: Implemented as D-zone-local behavior helper.

candidates.title: D-zone alert switcher component extraction
candidates.evidence: Implemented the shared-control part in W2 without extracting D-zone alert behavior. The alert switcher remains local in `DZoneSeasonInspector.tsx`; its buttons use `.ui-compact-control`/pressed utilities and local CSS now only owns D-zone layout emphasis.
candidates.next: No separate behavior extraction needed unless future D-zone inspectors reuse the same switcher outside this component.

candidates.title: D-zone history browser subcomponent split
candidates.evidence: History browser mixes modal behavior, focus, search, archive note, year groups, and season buttons.
candidates.next: Queue archive note/year group split after characterization.

candidates.title: D-zone relic and monster button extraction
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W5. `DZoneWaveCard` now has local `RelicButton`; the pre-existing local `MonsterButton` continues to own monster tile behavior. Collapsed accessibility, selected alert level chips, and lore text rendering remain covered by tests.
candidates.next: Implemented as D-zone-local root fix; C5 global media tile remains queued for terminal revisit.

candidates.title: Database card title and prioritized image primitive
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W4. Added `DatabaseGridCardTitle` and `shouldPrioritizeDatabaseGridImage`; migrated awakener, wheel, and simple artifact grid cards while preserving card frame behavior and first-24 eager image priority.
candidates.next: Implemented as database-internal root fix; no global card primitive extracted.

candidates.title: Database browse sort label config
candidates.evidence: Awakener and wheel sort label/direction helpers live inside the browse orchestration file.
candidates.next: Queue as a behavior-preserving config extraction.

candidates.title: Database mobile filter group extraction
candidates.evidence: Awakener and wheel filters duplicate mobile open state, ids, toggle, panel, and desktop rows.
candidates.next: Queue with UI/a11y checks for disclosure semantics.

candidates.title: Split database hybrid card measurement hook
candidates.evidence: `useMeasuredHybridCardMode` owns measurement, resize fallback, context value, and grid rendering in one file.
candidates.next: Queue as internal readability slice.

candidates.title: Database card accessible-name cleanup
candidates.evidence: Overlay button passes both `aria-label` and `aria-labelledby`; `aria-labelledby` wins.
candidates.next: Queue as a small a11y slice once tests can preserve accessible names.

candidates.title: Timeline page orchestration seam
candidates.evidence: TimelinePage combines URL state, data selection, D-zone parsing, detail modal wiring, masthead config, filters, and section layout.
candidates.next: Queue only after smaller Timeline helper slices reduce risk.

candidates.title: D-zone popover provider duplication
candidates.concept_blast_radius: src/pages/DZonePage.tsx; src/pages/DZoneHistoryPage.tsx; D-zone local provider helper
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W6. Added `DZonePopoverSurface` to own `useDZoneDatabasePopovers`, `DatabasePopoverContext.Provider`, and `DatabasePopoverRoot` wiring; current/history pages keep their markup and popover handlers.
candidates.next: Implemented as D-zone-local architecture cleanup.

maintenance.title: Token usage guardrail
maintenance.next: Consider after first token slice proves naming.

maintenance.title: Design-system documentation update
maintenance.next: Update DESIGN.md or local docs if implementation creates stable names.

tasks.title: Map cross-surface UI normalization candidates
protected_files: database detail modal internals unless shared primitive touches them
tasks.expected_output: Evidence-ranked candidates with concept blast radius, validation ideas, root-fix/patch notes, and maintenance candidates.

evidence: Database scout mapped browse/card/filter candidates and a11y risks.
Timeline scout mapped section headers, segmented controls, meta rows, motion/focus tokens, and the misnamed global scrollbar utility.
D-zone scout mapped local CSS tokens, alert switcher, media buttons, disclosure rows, and history-browser complexity.
Complexity and performance scans added C6, C7, and C8.

tasks.title: Choose first shared-token normalization slice
tasks.expected_output: One root-fix Worker slice for C1 plus C9, with local feature semantics preserved.

evidence: C1 was present in all three scout reports as repeated surface/focus/motion/control tokens.
C9 was explicitly reported by Timeline scout as `.database-scrollbar` used by Timeline and D-zone.
The selected slice avoids package files and database detail modal internals.
receipt.why_root_fix: D-zone already had good local tokens, Timeline and Database repeated the same values, and `.database-scrollbar` was a shared utility with the wrong feature name. Shared `--ui-*` tokens plus a neutral `ui-scrollbar` class remove cross-page reinvention without inventing a large component API.
receipt.what_not_to_change: No database detail modal internals, no Timeline banner drawer behavior, no D-zone wave layout, no dependency files.

tasks.title: Implement shared UI tokens and neutral scrollbar utility
tasks.expected_output: Shared `--ui-*` tokens, D-zone token mapping, Timeline/Database token use, neutral scrollbar migration with compatibility alias.

evidence: Added global `--ui-*` product-surface tokens in `src/index.css`.
Mapped `src/pages/d-zone/d-zone.css` local variables to shared tokens.
Migrated Timeline, Database, D-zone, and shared search scrollbar/motion/control styling to shared token or utility names.
receipt.what_got_simpler: One global SKeyDB product token vocabulary now supplies repeated panel, border, focus, label, radius, motion, and scrollbar values; D-zone keeps local semantic names as aliases; Timeline and Database use shared motion/control tokens instead of duplicating values.
receipt.behavior_preserved: No route, data, modal, card, drawer, or control semantics changed; only styling vocabulary and neutral utility class names changed.
receipt.current_complexity: Three surfaces had separate copies of the same product-surface constants and a global utility named for database.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: Shared constants live in `:root`; feature CSS keeps local layout and semantics.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root fix for the selected token/utility concept.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package files changed.
receipt.harness_update_considered: M1 and M2 remain queued after token names prove stable.
validation_run: npx vitest run src/pages/TimelinePage.test.tsx src/pages/timeline/TimelineArchiveSection.test.tsx src/pages/timeline/usePoolCycling.test.tsx src/pages/d-zone/DZoneWaveCard.test.tsx src/pages/DZonePage.test.tsx src/pages/DZoneHistoryPage.test.tsx src/features/database/DatabaseRoutes.test.tsx src/features/database/internal/DatabaseGrid.test.tsx
browser route checks for database, wheels, covenants, timeline, d-zone, and d-zone/history
receipt.risks: Small visual drift from shared label/control tokens; browser checks found no horizontal overflow.

tasks.title: Review W1 token/utility normalization slice
tasks.expected_output: Verdict, validation evidence, candidate register coverage, and next continuation prompt.

evidence: W1 preserved behavior and only changed styling token references plus utility class names.
Candidate register includes C1-C23; C1 and C9 are implemented, remaining in-scope candidates are queued rather than treated as complete.
No dependency files, AGENTS.md, Builder, Collection, or database detail modal internals were edited for this slice.

simplification_check.before: D-zone, Timeline, and Database repeated product-surface values independently; `.database-scrollbar` was a global utility with a feature-specific name.
simplification_check.after: Shared `--ui-*` tokens supply the first product-surface vocabulary; feature CSS keeps layout and semantic ownership; `.ui-scrollbar` is the neutral shared utility with compatibility alias.
simplification_check.net: Reduced token duplication and naming drift without creating a large shared component abstraction.

governance_check.focus_area_coverage: tranche covers extract/Tailwind token normalization; React/component, architecture, a11y, and complexity follow-ups remain queued
governance_check.root_fix_patch: root fix for selected token/utility concept
governance_check.characterization: existing tests plus browser checks sufficient for styling-token migration

validation: npm run lint passed after final code changes
npm run build:quiet passed after final code changes

tasks.title: Map next stack buckets for remaining cross-surface candidates
protected_files: database detail modal internals unless a selected shared primitive requires a tiny adapter
tasks.expected_output: Read-only evidence for grouping queued candidates into Worker-sized root-fix slices, including likely allowed files, characterization needs, and validation commands.

evidence: Shared vocabulary scout ranked C2+C14 compact pressed controls first, then C12/C19 disclosure rows, C4 metadata token, C3 section divider, and C21 database accessible-name cleanup.

D-zone scout ranked C16 local relic/monster button extraction, C23 popover provider duplication, C15/C6 history browser split, and C13 countdown helper; it advised keeping C5 queued until D-zone media tile behavior proves stable.

Timeline/database scout ranked C11 banner tag/pricing helper, C17 database card title/prioritized-image helper, C18 sort label config, C10 timeline section renderer, C20 card measurement hook, C7 BannerArtwork split, and C22 page orchestration seam.
All scouts agreed cross-surface extraction should stay vocabulary-sized unless repeated behavior semantics match.
characterization_notes: C2+C14 can rely on existing database route and D-zone alert tests, with browser checks for visual/focus state.
C12 needs D-zone history year expansion coverage before broad migration.
C7 needs characterization before splitting preload/detail-target/montage concerns.
C21 needs card accessible-name assertions before changing aria naming.

tasks.title: Choose compact pressed-control vocabulary slice
tasks.expected_output: One root-fix Worker slice for compact pressed controls and adjacent compact fields, preserving local D-zone alert behavior.

evidence: Database FilterChipButton, EntityViewControls sort controls, and D-zone alert buttons repeat the same compact dark/amber/focus vocabulary.
The behavior contracts differ, so the Worker may extract styling classes/tokens but must keep D-zone alert selection and database filter APIs local.
This is the clearest remaining cross-surface reuse reason after C1/C9.
receipt.why_root_fix: A shared compact control vocabulary removes duplicated full class strings and D-zone alert button state CSS without creating a behavior-level segmented-control abstraction.
receipt.what_not_to_change: No route/data semantics, no D-zone alert option model changes, no database filter state changes, no package files.

tasks.title: Implement compact pressed-control vocabulary
tasks.expected_output: Shared compact control CSS vocabulary, migrated FilterChipButton and database controls, D-zone alert switcher using shared pressed-control styling while preserving behavior.

evidence: Added `.ui-compact-control`, `.ui-compact-control--field`, `.ui-compact-control--pressed`, and `.ui-compact-control--dense` in `src/index.css`.
`FilterChipButton` now consumes the shared pressed-control vocabulary instead of carrying a full local Tailwind state string.
`EntityViewControls` sort select and direction button now consume shared compact field styling.
D-zone alert buttons now compose shared compact pressed-control classes; `d-zone.css` keeps only switcher layout/emphasis and no longer owns control border/background/focus/pressed state values.
receipt.what_got_simpler: Compact dark/amber pressed controls now have one shared styling vocabulary; database filters/sort controls and D-zone alert buttons no longer duplicate full state styling.
receipt.behavior_preserved: Filter chips still use `aria-pressed`; database sort controls keep the same select/toggle behavior; D-zone alert switcher remains local and still changes all waves to the selected alert.
receipt.current_complexity: At least three separate compact-control style definitions/classes across database and D-zone.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: One shared control utility family plus local layout classes.
receipt.data_access_render_invariants: No data loading, route, search, filter, or alert-selection semantics changed.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root fix for styling vocabulary; intentionally not a behavior-level segmented-control abstraction.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package files changed.
receipt.characterization: Existing focused route/component tests cover database pressed filters and D-zone alert switching; browser check covered rendered control sizing/font/radius.
receipt.harness_update_considered: M1/M2 remain queued; no new harness rule yet.
validation_run: npx vitest run src/features/database/DatabaseRoutes.test.tsx src/pages/DZonePage.test.tsx src/pages/d-zone/DZoneWaveCard.test.tsx -> 44 tests passed
browser checks for /database, /database/wheels, /d-zone -> no horizontal overflow; D-zone alert buttons visible, sans, 2px radius, and using shared compact-control classes
receipt.risks: Small visual drift from shared control colors and D-zone alert buttons moving from Droid Serif to sans, which matches the design canon for controls.
followups_not_done: Timeline price toggle can be migrated in a later Timeline-local slice if behavior remains compatible.
learning_lint_candidates: M2 design documentation update should include compact-control utility names once more slices settle.

tasks.title: Review compact pressed-control vocabulary slice
tasks.expected_output: Verdict, validation evidence, candidate register coverage, and next queued slice.

evidence: W2 reduced duplicated control styling without changing D-zone alert or database filter behavior.
The new shared abstraction is CSS vocabulary, not a forced cross-feature interaction component.
Focused tests, lint, build, diff check, packet checker, and browser checks passed.

simplification_check.before: Filter chips, database sort controls, and D-zone alert buttons repeated compact dark/amber/focus styling.
simplification_check.after: Shared `.ui-compact-control` utilities own compact control surface/pressed/focus states while features keep local layout and semantics.
simplification_check.net: Reduced CSS/Tailwind duplication and aligned controls with the SKeyDB sans-control canon.

governance_check.focus_area_coverage: covers C2/C14 plus Tailwind/CSS/a11y surface normalization; React/component and deeper complexity slices remain queued
governance_check.candidate_register: C2 and C14 implemented; remaining in-scope candidates stay queued
governance_check.root_fix_patch: root fix for selected styling-vocabulary concept
governance_check.characterization: existing tests plus browser checks sufficient

tasks.title: Choose Timeline banner metadata composition slice
tasks.expected_output: Timeline-local root fix for duplicated banner tag/custom-tag/pricing composition, with hero limit and drawer unlimited behavior preserved.

evidence: BannerCardHero and BannerInfoDrawer both compose display tags, custom tags, and formatted pricing.
The same tag labels/colors are used in two visual containers, but the hero limits to four items while the drawer remains unlimited.
A Timeline-local helper/component reduces duplication without creating a premature cross-surface metadata primitive.
receipt.why_root_fix: The repeated composition is a single Timeline concept with two direct consumers; local extraction removes duplicate logic while preserving distinct hero/drawer styling.
receipt.what_not_to_change: No banner artwork behavior, no drawer open/collapse behavior, no Timeline route/query behavior, no global metadata primitive.

tasks.title: Implement Timeline banner metadata helper
tasks.expected_output: Shared Timeline-local helper/component for banner tag/custom-tag/pricing metadata used by hero and drawer.

receipt.slice: Timeline-local banner metadata composition helper for C11.
evidence: Added `src/pages/timeline/bannerMetadata.tsx` with `BannerMetadataList` and local metadata item composition.
`BannerCardHero` delegates display/custom/pricing metadata to `BannerMetadataList` with `limit={4}`.
`BannerInfoDrawer` delegates the same metadata composition without a limit and keeps the empty metadata wrapper behavior with `renderWhenEmpty`.
receipt.what_got_simpler: The display tags + custom tags + formatted pricing composition now lives in one Timeline-local component rather than two render functions.
receipt.behavior_preserved: Hero tag order and four-item cap preserved; drawer remains uncapped; ended/active colors and separator colors preserved; pricing mode still controls formatted pricing.
receipt.current_complexity: Two direct consumers duplicated metadata item building and separator rendering.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: One Timeline helper owns metadata composition; hero and drawer only supply visual classes and limits.
receipt.data_access_render_invariants: No route, artwork, drawer open/collapse, detail-link, or pricing-domain semantics changed.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root fix for the Timeline banner metadata concept; not a global primitive.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package files changed.
receipt.characterization: Existing BannerCard tests cover pricing text, countdown/status, and artwork/detail interactions; TimelinePage test and browser check cover rendered route.
validation_run: npx vitest run src/pages/timeline/BannerCard.test.tsx -> passed in subagent, 14 tests
npx vitest run src/pages/timeline/BannerCard.test.tsx src/pages/TimelinePage.test.tsx -> 24 tests passed
browser check for /timeline -> no horizontal overflow; banner hero metadata rendered and drawer toggle present
receipt.risks: Low; helper prop surface is local to Timeline and carries visual class differences explicitly.
followups_not_done: C4 shared metadata token remains queued because Timeline banner metadata is richer than database card metadata.

tasks.title: Review Timeline banner metadata helper
tasks.expected_output: Verdict, validation evidence, and candidate register update.

evidence: W3 stayed within Timeline banner files and a new Timeline-local helper.

simplification_check.after: BannerMetadataList owns item composition and separator rendering; hero/drawer provide limit and visual classes.
simplification_check.net: Reduced duplicate React rendering logic while preserving distinct visual surfaces.

tasks.title: Choose database card title and priority helper slice
tasks.expected_output: Database-internal helper for repeated card title markup and prioritized image count across card types.

evidence:
Awakener, wheel, and simple artifact grid cards repeat the same title `

` markup and `PRIORITIZED_GRID_IMAGE_COUNT = 24` logic.

receipt.what_not_to_change: No card frame layout, modal/detail behavior, route behavior, or image asset selection semantics.

tasks.title: Implement database card title and priority helper

receipt.expected_complexity_after: One title component and one priority predicate used by all database browse card producers.

tasks.title: Choose D-zone popover surface wrapper slice
tasks.expected_output: D-zone-local wrapper for database popover provider/root wiring used by current and history pages.

evidence: DZonePage and DZoneHistoryPage both call `useDZoneDatabasePopovers`, provide `DatabasePopoverContext`, render page content, then render `DatabasePopoverRoot` with the same outside-click prop.
This is D-zone-local architecture cleanup around an existing database integration, not a shared database modal change.
receipt.why_root_fix: A small D-zone-local render-prop surface removes repeated provider/root wiring at the page boundary while preserving page markup and popover handlers.
receipt.what_not_to_change: No database popover internals, no D-zone wave behavior, no route/query behavior, no modal detail internals.

tasks.title: Implement D-zone popover surface wrapper
tasks.expected_output: D-zone current/history pages use one local wrapper for popover provider/root wiring while handlers and markup remain local.

receipt.slice: D-zone-local popover surface wrapper for C23.
evidence: Added `DZonePopoverSurface` to own `useDZoneDatabasePopovers`, `DatabasePopoverContext.Provider`, and `DatabasePopoverRoot` wiring.
`DZonePage` and `DZoneHistoryPage` now render their page content through the local wrapper render prop.
Page-owned route markup, selection/query behavior, and inspector handlers remain in the pages.
receipt.what_got_simpler: Current/history D-zone pages no longer duplicate the database popover provider/root shell.
receipt.behavior_preserved: Monster/relic popovers still use the same hook and outside-click preference; page routes, query params, history drawer behavior, and wave rendering are unchanged.
receipt.current_complexity: Two page components repeated the same provider/root wiring.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: One D-zone-local wrapper owns the integration shell.
receipt.data_access_render_invariants: No database popover internals, detail modal internals, route/query semantics, or D-zone wave data changed.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root fix at the D-zone page boundary; not a shared database primitive.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package files changed.
receipt.characterization: DZonePage and DZoneHistoryPage tests cover popover behavior, route/query behavior, and drawer interactions; browser checks covered both routes.
validation_run: npx vitest run src/pages/DZonePage.test.tsx src/pages/DZoneHistoryPage.test.tsx -> 16 tests passed
browser checks for /d-zone and /d-zone/history -> no overflow; pages and waves rendered
receipt.risks: Low; wrapper uses existing hook and render prop to avoid moving page logic.

tasks.title: Choose D-zone countdown helper slice
tasks.expected_output: One D-zone-local countdown helper used by current and history pages.

evidence: DZonePage has a local `getDzoneCountdownDisplay` and the history view model has equivalent active-only countdown logic.
The behavior is D-zone-specific but delegates to Timeline domain status/countdown helpers.
receipt.why_root_fix: A small D-zone helper removes duplicate active-only countdown logic while preserving page-specific rendering.
receipt.what_not_to_change: No route behavior, no date formatting behavior, no Timeline domain changes.

tasks.title: Implement shared D-zone countdown helper
tasks.expected_output: D-zone countdown helper plus migrated current/history callers and focused test coverage.

receipt.slice: Shared D-zone countdown helper for C13.
evidence: Added `getDZoneCountdownDisplay` in `src/pages/d-zone/d-zone-countdown.ts`.
`DZonePage` and `DZoneHistoryPage` now use the same active-only countdown helper.
Moved countdown behavior coverage from the history view-model test to `d-zone-countdown.test.ts`.
receipt.what_got_simpler: Active-only D-zone countdown behavior now lives in one helper instead of a page-local function and history view-model function.
receipt.behavior_preserved: Only active seasons return countdown text; ended seasons return an empty string; page rendering and date formatting remain unchanged.
receipt.current_complexity: Two separate functions implemented equivalent active-season checks.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: One helper used by current and history pages.
receipt.data_access_render_invariants: No route/query, season selection, or Timeline domain behavior changed.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root fix for the D-zone countdown concept.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package files changed.
receipt.characterization: Focused helper test pins active/ended behavior; D-zone page tests and browser checks cover integration.
validation_run: npx vitest run src/pages/d-zone/d-zone-countdown.test.ts src/pages/d-zone/d-zone-history-view-model.test.ts src/pages/DZonePage.test.tsx src/pages/DZoneHistoryPage.test.tsx -> 23 tests passed
browser checks for /d-zone and /d-zone/history -> no overflow; countdown and waves rendered
receipt.risks: Low; helper delegates to the same Timeline domain status/countdown functions.
